name: Gillyflower Sorbet
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a simple syrup with the water and sugar by bringing them to a boil and cooking over low heat until the sugar is dissolved.
2. Add gillyflowers (and lemon verbena, if using) and remove from heat. Let the mixture cool completely.
3. When cooled, pour into an ice cube tray without dividers and place in the freezer.
4. Once the sorbet forms solid ice crystals, transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl. Add the lemon juice (to taste) and egg white, then whip until frothy.
5. Put back into the tray and freeze again.
6. Just prior to serving, whip the sorbet one more time and garnish each serving with one perfect flower, preferably a gillyflower.
